steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
Left: An independent clause ending with “species.”
Right: A noun phrase with no main verb, beginning with “the former in a repository...”

steps.title: Apply the appositive rule
steps.content: A comma precedes an appositive noun phrase that elaborates on a preceding noun. Here, “the former in a repository at Washington, DC's Museum of Natural History and the latter in a repository at London's Natural History Museum” specifies where the specimens are preserved. Option A correctly uses a comma before the appositive. Options B, C, and D create errors: B adds an unwarranted “and,” C creates a sentence fragment, and D misapplies a semicolon to a noun phrase. That matches choice A — A is correct.
